'''Rick Geary''' (born [[February 25]], [[1940s#1946|1946]]) is an American cartoonist and illustrator, who wrote and pencilled "[[Luke Skywalker: Detective]]" from [[Star Wars Tales 20|''Star Wars Tales'' 20]].

==Biography==
'''Rick Geary''' was born on [[February 25]], [[1946]] in [[Wikipedia:Kansas City, Missouri|Kansas City]], Missouri. Geary was initially introduced to comics readers with his contributions to the [[Wikipedia:Heavy Metal (magazine)|''Heavy Metal'']] and ''National Lampoon'' magazines.

Geary's distinctive cartooning style evolved from his early imitations of [[Wikipedia:Edward Gorey|Edward Gorey]]. His drawings typically consist of stark clean black lines against a white background, with a total absence of half-tone or shading. Even more distinctive is Geary's method of panel art. Most comics artists will draw several consecutive sequential panels of the same characters in the same setting: Geary, uniquely, almost never devotes two consecutive panels to the same locale or character. This creates a constant impression of jumping from one image to another.

Geary has drawn a variety of solo comic books and graphic novels for various publishers, including an adaptation of [[Wikipedia:Great Expectations|''Great Expectations'']] for the revived Classics Illustrated series and a kid-oriented [[Wikipedia:Flaming Carrot|Flaming Carrot]] spinoff. His most extensive project is his ongoing non-fiction comic book series, ''A Treasury of Victorian Murder''. The series chronicles such 19th century criminals as [[Wikipedia:H. H. Holmes|H. H. Holmes]], [[Wikipedia:Lizzie Borden|Lizzie Borden]], [[Wikipedia:Charles Guiteau|Charles Guiteau]] and [[Wikipedia:Jack the Ripper|Jack the Ripper]]. In the series he often uses literary devices characteristic of 19th century popular literature. For example, ''The Borden Tragedy'' is narrated through excerpts of a period diary, and ''The Fatal Bullet'' didactically contrasts the lives and morality of Guiteau and his victim, President [[Wikipedia:James Garfield|James Garfield]].

The [[Wikipedia:National Cartoonist Society|National Cartoonist Society]] awarded Geary its Magazine and Book Illustration Award in 1994.
